title: Innovation Fair & Web Launch
subtitle: Demo Projects. View Class Website.
overview: The grand finale where students showcase their innovative STEAM projects at the Innovation Fair and officially launch their custom-built class website to the public.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — project demo photos, web launch event shots, student presentations, showcase gallery.

keyTopics: Project Demonstration — Presenting prototypes clearly to an audience
Web Deployment — Transitioning a project from local code to a live public URL
Feedback Loops — Gathering structured peer reviews and guest feedback
Public Speaking — Explaining complex tech concepts in simple terms
STEAM Integration — Celebrating the intersection of design, code, and engineering

youtubeVideos.title: How to Present a STEAM Project Professionally
youtubeVideos._note: Replace videoId with actual YouTube video ID from your fair summary video if available
youtubeVideos.desc: Tips and tricks for students on how to pitch their inventions and present their websites confidently to tech judges.

resources.title: GitHub Pages — Host Your Project Website Free

activity.title: Live Project Exhibition & Website Unveiling
parts: Projector or Monitor for Website Launch
Feedback Forms / QR Codes for Guest Reviews
steps: Set up the project booths with working hardware and posters
Load up the class web application onto the main display terminal
Initiate the 'Launch Countdown' and push the project repository live
Conduct live 3-minute lightning demos for visitors and class peers
Collect audience feedback and iterate based on user interactions

quiz.q: What is the primary purpose of an 'Innovation Fair'?
options: To demonstrate working projects, pitch creative solutions, and share knowledge
To sit quietly and watch a movie
quiz.explanation: An Innovation Fair is designed to celebrate creative problem-solving by showcasing functional prototypes and sharing concepts with a live community audience!

quiz.q: What does a 'Web Launch' mean in practical technology terms?
options: Deploying your local project code to a public server so anyone can access it via a URL
quiz.explanation: Web Launch means taking your files online! Your website becomes live on the global internet, moving from local staging to user-facing production.

quiz.q: Why is gathering user and peer feedback crucial during a project launch?
options: It helps identify bugs, improvements, and validates if the product solves a real problem
It acts as a permanent grade penalty
It ensures nobody else can copy your design ideas
It marks the absolute end of the project without any future changes
quiz.explanation: Iterative engineering relies heavily on feedback loops! User data allows developers to polish UI/UX elements and fix unexpected software vulnerabilities.
